title: COSO Principle 1 — Commitment to integrity: ethics-hotline and code-of-conduct violation monitoring
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=COSO, clause=Principle1, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for Principle1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that COSO Principle 1 (Commitment to integrity and ethical values) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.31: COSO Principle 1 — Commitment to integrity: ethics-hotline and code-of-conduct violation monitoring.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.31' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: Ethics-hotline system exports, HR case-management logs, code-of-conduct acknowledgement records.
spl:
index=hr_compliance sourcetype IN (ethics_hotline, conduct_violations) earliest=-90d
| eval resolved=if(status IN ("closed", "resolved", "remediated"), 1, 0)
| eval days_to_resolve=if(resolved=1, round((resolution_time - _time)/86400), round((now() - _time)/86400))
| stats count AS total_reports sum(resolved) AS resolved_count avg(days_to_resolve) AS avg_resolution_days dc(reporter_department) AS departments_reporting values(category) AS categories BY severity
| eval open_count=total_reports - resolved_count
| eval resolution_pct=round(100*resolved_count/total_reports, 1)
| where open_count > 0 OR resolution_pct < 90 OR avg_resolution_days > 30
| table severity, total_reports, resolved_count, open_count, resolution_pct, avg_resolution_days, departments_reporting, categories
description: Monitors ethics and conduct reporting effectiveness as evidence for COSO Principle 1 (commitment to integrity and ethical values). Adds exception-based filtering to surface only cases where open reports exist, resolution rates fall below 90%, or average resolution exceeds 30 days.
value: COSO Principle 1 requires the organisation to demonstrate commitment to integrity and ethical values. Continuous monitoring of ethics reports and their resolution provides auditable evidence of tone-at-the-top.
implementation: (1) Ingest ethics-hotline and conduct-violation events with category, severity, status, resolution_time; (2) schedule monthly; (3) trend analysis for board reporting. Indexes required: `index=hr_compliance`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=conduct_violations`, `sourcetype=ethics_hotline`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `avg_resolution_days`, `categories`, `days_to_resolve`, `departments_reporting`, `open_count`, `resolution_pct`.
knownFalsePositives: Tier-2 framework drift during quarterly reviews, scheduled crosswalk updates, or controlled exception cycles.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Ethics-hotline system exports, HR case-management logs, code-of-conduct acknowledgement records.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest ethics-hotline and conduct-violation events with category, severity, status, resolution_time; (2) schedule monthly; (3) trend analysis for board reporting.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hr_compliance sourcetype IN (ethics_hotline, conduct_violations) earliest=-90d
| eval resolved=if(status IN ("closed", "resolved", "remediated"), 1, 0)
| eval days_to_resolve=if(resolved=1, round((resolution_time - _time)/86400), round((now() - _time)/86400))
| stats count AS total_reports sum(resolved) AS resolved_count avg(days_to_resolve) AS avg_resolution_days dc(reporter_department) AS departments_reporting values(category) AS categories BY severity
| eval open_count=total_reports - resolved_count
| eval resolution_pct=round(100*resolved_count/total_reports, 1)
| where open_count > 0 OR resolution_pct < 90 OR avg_resolution_days > 30
| table severity, total_reports, resolved_count, open_count, resolution_pct, avg_resolution_days, departments_reporting, categories
```

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
grandmaExplanation: We help you show how a second-line framework line maps to what you actually monitor, so your gap story stays clear.
